_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about different historical interpretations and debates about Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples' rights and freedoms.

_source.w: We are learning about different historical interpretations and debates about Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples' rights and freedoms.
y: These debates are among the most significant and contested in Australian historiography.
Engaging with these interpretations develops our capacity for critical thinking about Australia's past and present.
The way this history is told — and by whom — has profound implications for reconciliation and justice.
eg: Investigating the 'history wars' and how historians have debated the nature and extent of frontier violence.
Analysing different interpretations of the stolen generations — from denial to recognition.
Examining how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander historians and scholars have challenged mainstream narratives.
Discussing the significance of truth-telling commissions in addressing contested histories.
Evaluating different perspectives on constitutional recognition and treaty — what is at stake in these debates.
Presenting our own informed position on a contested aspect of this history.
